channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: .NET 10 Performance: The O(n^2) String Trap and the Zero-Allocation Quest
item.description: "Premature optimization is the root of all evil." We’ve all heard it. But in the world of high-load cloud systems and serverless environments, there is another truth: "Ignoring scalability is the...

item.title: Few treding github repositories

item.title: Oeuf is a punishing platformer in a cozy shell
item.description: The funny shape of eggs is the curious lifeblood of Oeuf, the new physics platformer by prolific developer Increpare Games. In a gaming landscape saturated with complex systems dropped into simple...

item.title: My journey with Owasp and Open Source
item.description: For the longest time, contributing to open-source software was something I only dreamt about. I would read about developers collaborating globally, building tools that millions use, and I knew I...

item.title: Payroll Engine: From Open-Source Prototype to Production-Ready
item.description: Two and a half years ago, I introduced Payroll Engine in this series — an open-source payroll calculation framework written in C#. The architecture was in place, regulation layers worked, the...

item.title: The 7 GitHub Repos That Will Actually Get You Hired
item.description: Let's be honest: the developer job hunt is brutal. You're told to "learn to code," "build projects," and "grind LeetCode." But where do you even start? It's easy to waste months on tutorials that...

item.title: OSINT Isn’t About Skill Anymore. It’s About Systems
item.description: The profile looked clean. Too clean. No messy posts from five years ago. No awkward phase. No late-night arguments that should have been deleted but weren’t. Just a smooth timeline, polished like...

item.title: Why AI "Solving Coding" Might Actually Be the Industry's Next Nightmare
item.description: The buzz in 2026 is everywhere: "Coding is solved." We’re hearing it from AI CEOs and VCs, even while their own projects are sitting on thousands of open bugs. The dream sounds amazing: autonomous...

item.title: Dream Interpretation with AI: What Dreaming About Snakes Actually Means
item.description: { "title": "Building an AI Dream Interpreter: Why I Dreamed of Snakes and What It Really Meant", "description": "I built a dream interpreter using LangGraph and Claude. Here is how we analyze...

item.title: How I Built a Business Email Agent with Compliance Controls in Go
item.description: Every few weeks another AI agent product launches that can "handle your email." Dispatch, OpenClaw, and a dozen others promise to read, summarize, and reply on your behalf. They work fine for...

item.title: Automatically Committing Image Tags with Argo CD Image Updater
item.description: In GitOps workflows using Argo CD, automating container image updates is essential. In this article, we will walk through how to set up Argo CD Image Updater in practice based on the following...

item.title: Dreame’s self-cleaning L10s Pro Ultra is nearly $1,000 off its original list price
item.description: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale doesn’t kick off until next week, but the retailer is already dropping a host of good deals, especially on robot vacuums. We recently saw Ecovacs’ Deebot X8 and X9 Pro...

item.title: The new MacBook Pro is still fast as hell
item.description: Apple's flagship 16-inch MacBook Pro has reigned supreme in the world of creator-focused laptops since its M-series processor overhaul in 2021. Since then, we've mostly seen the same design with...

item.title: An early contender for movie of the year
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 120, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, get ready for Pen Opinions, and also you can read all the old...

item.title: Gemini task automation is slow, clunky, and super impressive
item.description: I've been testing out Gemini's new task automation on the Pixel 10 Pro and the Galaxy S26 Ultra, which for the first time lets Gemini take the wheel and use apps for you. It's limited to a small...

item.title: I Tried DoorDash’s Tasks App and Saw the Bleak Future of AI Gig Work
item.description: I recorded videos of myself doing laundry, scrambling eggs, and walking around the park in DoorDash’s new Tasks app, where gig workers are paid to train AI.

item.title: Kodiak CEO says making trucks drive themselves is only half the battle
item.description: This year is shaping up to be a big one for self-driving trucks. In addition to Aurora's plan to deploy hundreds of autonomous big rigs and Waabi expanding into robotaxis, you've also got Kodiak...

item.title: Cyberattack on a Car Breathalyzer Firm Leaves Drivers Stuck
item.description: Plus: The FBI admits it’s buying phone data to track Americans, Iranian hackers disrupt medical care at Maryland hospitals, and more.

item.title: Oracle Patches Critical CVE-2026-21992 Enabling Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
item.description: Oracle has released security updates to address a critical security flaw impacting Identity Manager and Web Services Manager that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution. The...

item.title: 'Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat' Almost Makes Corporate Culture Seem Fun
item.description: The Amazon Prime prank series amplifies the hijinks of workplace dynamics, while showing how people find purpose—and community—in their jobs despite impossible situations.

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Triggers Self-Spreading CanisterWorm Across 47 npm Packages
item.description: The threat actors behind the supply chain attack targeting the popular Trivy scanner are suspected to be conducting follow-on attacks that have led to the compromise of a large number of npm...

item.title: CISA Flags Apple, Craft CMS, Laravel Bugs in KEV, Orders Patching by April 3, 2026
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added five security flaws impacting Apple, Craft CMS, and Laravel Livewire to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV)...

item.title: The improved battery-powered Starlink Mini is here
item.description: My Starlink Mini has radically changed the way I live. It's the biggest reason I can work remotely from my converted van, providing fast and reliable internet in places that traditional 4G and 5G...

item.title: New court filing reveals Pentagon told Anthropic the two sides were nearly aligned — a week after Trump declared the relationship kaput
item.description: Anthropic submitted two sworn declarations to a California federal court late Friday afternoon, pushing back on the Pentagon's assertion that the AI company poses an "unacceptable risk to national...

item.title: Anthropic Denies It Could Sabotage AI Tools During War
item.description: The Department of Defense alleges the AI developer could manipulate models in the middle of war. Company executives argue that’s impossible.

item.title: Jury finds Elon Musk’s ‘stupid tweets’ caused Twitter investors’ losses
item.description: A California jury determined that Elon Musk misled Twitter investors before making a $44 billion deal to buy the company in 2022, reports CNBC. The New York Times reports that Musk had testified...

item.title: There Aren’t a Lot of Reasons to Get Excited About a New Amazon Smartphone
item.description: The company is reportedly building a new AI-powered mobile device. If Amazon follows through on the plan, experts warn it would be next to impossible to break into a crowded market.

item.title: An automated moderation error left Tumblr users panicked
item.description: Tumblr users were left scrambling on Wednesday after dozens of accounts were banned in the same afternoon by an automated system. Numerous users contacted The Verge about the incident, claiming...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jumbo Flying Squid in the South Pacific
item.description: The population needs better conservation. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Microsoft rolls back some of its Copilot AI bloat on Windows
item.description: The company is reducing Copilot entry points on Windows, starting with Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and other apps.
